description_long: This object is a dagger-like knife with a straight, double-edged blade made of metallic material, tapering to a sharp point. The blade is flanked by a prominent, curved crossguard that extends widely on both sides, providing hand protection and a distinctive silhouette. The handle is cylindrical and appears to be wrapped or textured for grip, ending in a rounded pommel. The overall design is utilitarian with no visible ornamentation, suggesting a focus on function over decoration. The metallic blade has a matte, slightly reflective finish, while the handle is darker and likely made of a composite or polymer material. The proportions and construction indicate it is intended for hand-held use, suitable for cutting or thrusting tasks.
description: A straight, double-edged dagger with a wide curved crossguard and a textured cylindrical handle, finished in metallic and dark tones.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the blade, the wide curved crossguard, and the cylindrical handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blade and handle, with the crossguard appearing as a narrow line.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the blade, crossguard, and handle from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile of the blade and handle, similar to view 1.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, textured or wrapped, dark polymer, rounded pommel
